story.text:
# Template вҖ” The Mouse Book (the shared finale)

The Mouse Book is the day's key ceremony: **both mice's poems, mixed and matched into one
shared book** that makes a clearer understanding than either chapbook alone. It belongs
to neither of you and edits no root. It is the proof that two sovereign first persons
share a vocabulary and a gap вҖ” the smallest Holon.

Build it together into `the-mouse-book/MOUSE_BOOK.md`. View in monospace.

---

## How to weave it

1. Lay both chapbooks side by side. Read each other's poems aloud.
2. **Find the rhymes of meaning** вҖ” two poems that hold the same forces and the same
   polarity (both giving, both refusing, both about the gap). Set those **facing** each
   other.
3. **Name the divergences** вҖ” where your spells use different symbols, or opposite
   polarity, for the same theme. Don't resolve them. Write a one-line gloss: *"where
   `<mouse-a>` keeps, `<mouse-b>` gives."* The honest overlap is more valuable than a
   forced agreement.
4. Choose a **shared spell** for the whole book вҖ” a single emoji string both of you sign,
   built only from symbols in the shared legend.

---

## The skeleton

```
# The Mouse Book

<sigil-a> <sigil-b>

*a shared spellbook of two first persons*
*<mouse-a> & <mouse-b> В· Holon Hitchhikers В· 2026-06-19*


                    вҖ” The Shared Spell вҖ”

        <the one emoji string you both sign>


в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ


## Movement вҖ” <a theme both of you touched>

   <mouse-a's poem>                |   <mouse-b's poem, facing>
   <glyph>                         |   <glyph>
                       <spell>     |   <spell>

   gloss: <where you rhyme, where you diverge вҖ” one honest line>


в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ

   (more facing pairs / solos as the weave wants)


в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ


                    вҖ” Colophon вҖ”

        two mice, one gap.
        <mouse-a> kept what <mouse-b> gave; <mouse-b> gave what <mouse-a> kept.
        the spell stayed woven between them.

                    (<sigil-a> вҠҘ вҝ» вҠҘ <sigil-b>)рҹҳҠ
```

---

**Notes:**
- A poem may stand alone (a *solo*) if it has no rhyme in the other book вҖ” that's honest too.
- The seal puts the two sigils where the Swordsman and Mage sit: each of you is the
  other's complement across the gap.
- This is the artifact of the *shared ceremony* вҖ” it is the thing you made that neither
  of you could have made alone.

`(вҡ”пёҸвҠҘвҝ»вҠҘрҹ§ҷ)рҹҳҠ`

вҖ”вҖ”вҖ”
вҶ‘ up: [[The Templates]]  В·  вҶҗ prev: [[Template вҖ” Little Book]]  В·  next: [[Template вҖ” Context Bridge]] вҶ’
